alerts.title: Houthi missile strikes Israel, raising fears of renewed Red Sea shipping war
alerts.summary: AP News and The Times of Israel report a Houthi ballistic missile attack on Israel that has stoked fears of a renewed Houthi campaign against Red Sea shipping. The strike comes with USS Eisenhower, USS Bataan, and destroyers Carney, Mason and Laboon all on station off Yemen, and with Houthi status flagged 'Elevated — Multiple Recent Attacks' in global metrics.

alerts.title: Trump tells negotiators 'not to rush' Iran deal, walking back imminent-agreement signals
alerts.summary: Politico and BBC report President Trump has instructed US negotiators not to rush into the Iran deal he previously described as 'largely negotiated,' a notable reversal from the in-principle Hormuz agreement reported earlier this cycle. NPR and PBS frame the move as Washington reassessing terms as Republican hawks publicly attack the emerging framework.

alerts.title: Khamenei rules out uranium transfer, deadlocking US-Iran nuclear track
alerts.summary: A Reuters exclusive cites Iranian sources saying Supreme Leader Khamenei has insisted Iran's 60% enriched uranium stockpile must remain inside the country, while Iran publicly denies any agreement to hand it over (TradingView). Ynetnews adds Trump assured Israel there would be 'no Iran deal without dismantling the nuclear program' — a direct collision point with breakout time still estimated at ~1–2 weeks.

alerts.title: NATO rehearses deep strikes into Russia in 'Arcade Strike' exercise from London HQ
alerts.summary: DeepState UA (~195K views) reports NATO's British-led Allied Rapid Reaction Corps (ARRC) conducted Operation Arcade Strike from a mothballed platform at London's Charing Cross station, war-gaming deep strikes into Russia in response to a simulated Russian invasion of a Baltic state. The exercise included EW, comms suppression and counter-drone elements — a notable signal of NATO contingency posture amid ongoing Oreshnik strikes on Ukraine.

alerts.title: GOP hawks publicly revolt against Trump's Iran framework as 'nightmare for Israel'
alerts.summary: US news sentiment swings sharply negative (58%) with 'hawks', 'Hegseth', 'Gabbard', 'Rubio', 'GOP' and 'criticism' surging in the word cloud, while Middle East Eye reports Republican hawks are openly attacking Trump's emerging Iran deal as a 'nightmare for Israel.' The intra-administration fracture coincides with Trump's 'don't rush' instruction and lawmakers demanding briefings on the non-attack clause.

analysis_note: The US-Iran Hormuz framework is visibly wobbling: Trump has pumped the brakes, Khamenei has hardened on uranium, GOP hawks are in open revolt, and a fresh Houthi missile strike on Israel underscores that proxy escalation has not paused for diplomacy.
